page: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g-240s is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4 shared vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core, alongside 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a bundled 240 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this server type.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities alongside moderate multi-core and memory throughput. The AMD EPYC-Turin processor achieves top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression tests. However, multi-core execution shows some limitations, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores falling into the average tier, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression registering in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth remains stable in the average tier, reaching 440.06 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 70.96 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation makes this instance a budget-friendly option for workloads that do not require dedicated, continuous CPU execution. The inclusion of fast local NVMe SSD storage and a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides solid value for general-purpose applications. It is well-suited for static web serving, Redis database operations, and lightweight background processing. It is less optimal for heavy multi-threaded compute tasks or medium-to-large language model inference due to its shared CPU architecture and average multi-core scaling.
description: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g-240s is a general-purpose virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is equipped with 4 shared vCPUs on an x86_64 AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and a 240 GB NVMe SSD. While single-core benchmarks for compression and stress-ng place this server in the top tier, its multi-core performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average category, with multi-threaded decompression showing some performance trade-offs. The shared CPU allocation positions this instance as a cost-efficient option for workloads that do not demand sustained, dedicated CPU resources. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, Redis key-value stores, and lightweight utility tasks.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g-240s is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 16 GB of RAM, and a 240 GB NVMe SSD, offering balanced performance for standard workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g-240s is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 240 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and fast local NVMe SSD storage for everyday workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC-Turin x86_64 processor with 4 shared vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Bundled 240 GB local NVMe SSD storage for fast read and write operations
Top-tier single-core performance in compression and stress-ng benchmarks
Cost-efficient shared CPU allocation suitable for non-continuous workloads
Appropriate for static web serving, Redis databases, and general-purpose applications
